name: motor
description: DC motor control via L298N H-bridge driver. Speed control via PWM, direction via two GPIO pins. For simple on/off, a relay or MOSFET also works.

parts.name: L298N motor driver
parts.spec: Dual H-bridge, up to 2A per channel

parts.name: External power
parts.spec: 6-12V DC (battery or adapter matching motor voltage)

parts.name: Optional: capacitor
parts.spec: 100µF electrolytic across motor terminals for noise reduction

wiring.L298N_GND: External power GND AND ESP32 GND (common ground!)
wiring.L298N_5V: Option A: ESP32 5V pin (if jumper on L298N 5V-EN is set). Option B: leave disconnected if using >12V.
wiring.L298N_ENA: ESP32 GPIO (PWM speed) — jumper must be REMOVED from L298N ENA pins
wiring.diagram:
External Power(+) → L298N 12V
External Power(-) → L298N GND → ESP32 GND
ESP32 GPIO(D1)  → L298N IN1
ESP32 GPIO(D2)  → L298N IN2
ESP32 GPIO(PWM) → L298N ENA (with jumper removed)
L298N OUT1 → Motor(+)
L298N OUT2 → Motor(-)

esp32_constraints.pwm_channels: ESP32 has 16 independent PWM channels. Each motor uses 1 channel.
esp32_constraints.gpio_choice: Any output-capable GPIO for IN1/IN2. Any PWM-capable GPIO for ENA.
esp32_constraints.power_isolation: Motor power MUST be separate from ESP32 power. Only GND is shared.
esp32_constraints.noise: DC motors generate electrical noise. Add 100µF capacitor across motor terminals. Avoid long parallel runs of motor and signal wires.

code.example:
from machine import Pin, PWM
import time

class DCMotor:
    def __init__(self, in1_pin, in2_pin, en_pin, freq=1000):
        self.in1 = Pin(in1_pin, Pin.OUT)
        self.in2 = Pin(in2_pin, Pin.OUT)
        self.en = PWM(Pin(en_pin), freq=freq, duty=0)

    def forward(self, speed):
        """speed: 0-1023 (10-bit PWM)"""
        self.in1.on()
        self.in2.off()
        self.en.duty(speed)

    def backward(self, speed):
        """speed: 0-1023"""
        self.in1.off()
        self.in2.on()
        self.en.duty(speed)

    def stop(self):
        self.en.duty(0)
        self.in1.off()
        self.in2.off()

    def brake(self):
        """Short brake — motor resists movement."""
        self.in1.on()
        self.in2.on()
        self.en.duty(0)

    def deinit(self):
        self.en.deinit()

# Usage
motor = DCMotor(in1_pin=25, in2_pin=26, en_pin=27)

motor.forward(512)   # half speed forward
time.sleep(2)
motor.stop()
time.sleep(0.5)
motor.backward(768)  # 75% speed backward
time.sleep(2)
motor.brake()
motor.deinit()
code.pwm_note: PWM frequency 100-1000Hz works well. Below 100Hz = audible whine. Above 20kHz = silent but may reduce torque.

l298n_channel2.description: L298N has two channels (A and B). Use the same pattern for IN3/IN4/ENB on the second channel.
l298n_channel2.pins: IN3, IN4 (direction) + ENB (PWM speed) — same wiring pattern as channel A.

alternative_drivers.l293d: Similar to L298N but lower current (600mA). Same pin logic.
alternative_drivers.drv8833: Smaller, 1.5A, dual H-bridge. Same control logic.
alternative_drivers.mosfet_irlz44n: Single transistor for unidirectional control. PWM on gate. Simpler but no reverse.
alternative_drivers.relay: On/off only, no speed control. Use relay module with optocoupler.

pitfalls: FORGETTING common GND between ESP32 and L298N — motor won't spin, possible damage.
Leaving ENA jumper on — PWM won't work (motor always at full speed). REMOVE the jumper cap.
Motor voltage too high for L298N (>12V may overheat). Check motor specs and L298N datasheet.
Duty cycle of 0 = motor off. Very low duty (<100) may not overcome static friction — motor hums but doesn't spin.
brake() shorts motor terminals through H-bridge — motor resists turning. Do NOT use brake() for long periods (heats up driver).
ESP32 PWM channels are shared — if another peripheral uses the same channel, motor may behave erratically.
